channel.title: SEA Food & Agriculture Digest
channel.description: Food and agriculture news from across Southeast Asia — smallholders, cooperatives, corporates, and the regional protein debate.

item.title: ADB commits $200 million to Mekong Delta agroecology facility structured around farmer-led cooperatives
item.description: The Asian Development Bank has announced a $200 million facility targeting agroecological transition across Vietnam, Cambodia, and Laos rice smallholder systems. All capital will be allocated through farmer-led cooperative structures over three years, with ADB's Manila office naming four anchor cooperative federations as initial disbursement partners. The facility is ADB's first dedicated agroecology vehicle in Asia and sits explicitly outside the bank's existing sustainable-agriculture portfolio.

item.title: Minh Phu becomes first Vietnamese seafood exporter to commit entire supply chain to regenerative aquaculture
item.description: Minh Phu has committed to converting its entire shrimp supply chain to regenerative aquaculture principles by 2027, backed by a $40 million transition investment and binding off-take terms with coastal smallholder cooperatives across five Mekong provinces. The commitment explicitly rejects high-density pond models in favour of mangrove-integrated systems. Minh Phu is the first major Asian seafood exporter to treat transition lock-in as a board-level risk and to put capital behind that framing.

item.title: Unilever commits to 50 per cent agroecological sourcing across Southeast Asian agricultural inputs by 2030
item.description: Unilever's regional unit has committed to sourcing 50 per cent of its Southeast Asian agricultural inputs from agroecological systems by 2030, with third-party verification handled by Bureau Veritas. The commitment covers coconut, dairy, palm oil, and spice supply sheds across Indonesia, Malaysia, the Philippines, and Vietnam. Quarterly progress reporting will include cooperative-origin volume share alongside hectare-based metrics.

item.title: Olam expands Indonesian cacao cooperative sourcing to 40 per cent with Sulawesi agroforestry programme
item.description: Olam Group has committed to sourcing 40 per cent of its Indonesian cacao volume from smallholder cooperatives by Q4 2026, up from 22 per cent today. The programme includes a 2,300-member agroforestry commitment in South Sulawesi, a 12 per cent farmgate premium tied to verified agroforestry practice, and joint investment in cooperative-owned cold-chain infrastructure. Olam has framed the shift as a deliberate move away from commodity-broker sourcing toward smallholder-centred direct trade.

item.title: Charoen Pokphand publishes Thai poultry transition lock-in assessment and commits to 25 per cent industrial-density reduction
item.description: Charoen Pokphand's Thai poultry unit has published an internal transition lock-in self-assessment covering its domestic broiler supply chain, a first among major Asian integrators. CP has committed to reducing industrial-density stocking by 25 per cent over four years and to shifting a quarter of contract-grower volume toward lower-density cooperative-tier operations. The document uses the term transition lock-in directly, adopting vocabulary more commonly associated with investor critique than corporate reporting.

item.title: GFI APAC consumer research finds 12 per cent annual decline in Southeast Asian alternative-protein interest
item.description: GFI APAC's latest regional consumer-research report documents a 12 per cent year-on-year decline in Southeast Asian consumer interest in alternative-protein products, a result the institute itself describes as challenging to the sector's investment thesis. The report draws on 4,800 respondents across six countries and explicitly recommends investors recalibrate growth assumptions baked into current climate-smart protein fund models. It is a rare public concession from within the alt-protein ecosystem that demand trajectory has softened.

item.title: Philippines opens national agroecology strategy consultation with regional workshops
item.description: The Philippine Department of Agriculture has opened a formal public consultation on a draft national agroecology strategy, with workshops scheduled across all 17 administrative regions through the next three months. The consultation draft uses the term agroecology throughout, rather than the country's prior climate-smart agriculture framing, and names smallholder equity as one of four strategic pillars. Cabinet sign-off is targeted for Q3.

item.title: Cambodian smallholder federation expands cooperative network to 18 villages in northern provinces
item.description: A Cambodian smallholder federation based in Preah Vihear has completed the integration of six additional village cooperatives, bringing its active network to 18 villages across the northern provinces. Federation leadership says the expanded footprint will allow a bid for pooled storage infrastructure financing from a regional rural development fund later this year. The federation is one of a small handful of Cambodian cooperative bodies operating at multi-district scale.

item.title: Nestle Malaysia begins regenerative dairy pilot across 380 smallholder farms
item.description: Nestle's Malaysian operation has begun a regenerative dairy sourcing pilot covering roughly 380 smallholder farms organised under two cooperative structures across Negeri Sembilan and Pahang. The pilot pays a premium tied to verified soil-health and pasture-rotation metrics, with third-party measurement running over 24 months before a full programme decision. It is positioned as a test-bed for cooperative-origin dairy at commercial scale in Malaysia.

item.title: Indonesia circulates draft palm oil policy including smallholder-equity provisions
item.description: Indonesia's Ministry of Agriculture has circulated a draft palm oil policy framework for stakeholder consultation, with explicit smallholder-equity provisions including co-investment thresholds and cooperative-tier procurement quotas. The draft sits within a broader review triggered by compliance demands from the EU deforestation regulation and by domestic concern over smallholder price exposure. It represents the first time smallholder-equity language has appeared in a Ministry-level palm oil draft.

item.title: Lao coffee cooperative secures $14m ADB blended-finance facility for agroforestry transition
item.description: The Asian Development Bank has closed a $14 million blended-finance facility with a Bolaven Plateau coffee cooperative, financing a shade-tree and intercropping conversion across 3,100 hectares. The facility puts long-duration concessional capital at the cooperative level rather than at a corporate off-taker — an architecture ADB staff described as closer to system finance than conventional supply-chain lending. First disbursements cover nursery establishment and cooperative governance capacity.

item.title: Hanoi-based regional outlet launches Southeast Asian sustainable agriculture reporting vertical
item.description: A Hanoi-based regional news outlet has launched a dedicated Southeast Asian sustainable agriculture reporting vertical, with initial coverage commissioned across rice, coffee, and aquaculture supply chains. The launch is being read by regional editors as a signal that sustainable-agriculture coverage has reached a sustained editorial audience rather than being treated as occasional feature material.

item.title: Yangon University paper examines smallholder inclusion in Myanmar pulse export chains
item.description: A working paper from Yangon University's agricultural economics department examines the structural barriers faced by Myanmar mungbean and chickpea smallholders in accessing direct export markets. The paper draws on field data from three regions and argues that cooperative legal-form reform, not technical training, is the binding constraint. It joins a small but growing body of Myanmar-focused academic work on cooperative structure.

item.title: Food security rises on upcoming ASEAN ministerial 2026 agenda
item.description: The upcoming ASEAN agriculture ministers' meeting has listed food security and systems resilience among its five priority agenda items, up from a secondary placement last year. Officials working on the preparatory drafts say the shift reflects member-state concern over compound climate and supply-chain shocks rather than a specific sector commitment. No binding ministerial text is expected before the mid-year summit.

item.title: Southeast Asian ESG disclosure requirements tighten for listed agribusiness
item.description: Revised ESG disclosure requirements affecting listed agribusinesses have taken effect across three SEA exchanges, mandating quantitative reporting on deforestation exposure and smallholder-sourcing share. The requirements do not prescribe target levels and are expected to produce the first wave of comparable baseline data by the end of 2026.

item.title: Singapore cultivated-meat startup closes $85 million Series C for lab-grown chicken
item.description: A Singapore-based cultivated-meat startup has closed an $85 million Series C round led by a consortium of climate-smart protein funds, with plans to expand into South Korean retail. Executives described the round as further proof that alternative proteins remain the central pathway for Asian protein transition, and pointed to Singapore's permissive regulator as the enabling backdrop.

item.title: Asian beverage company launches plant-based milk line across Malaysian retail
item.description: A regional beverage company has launched a new plant-based milk line across 600 Malaysian retail outlets, with oat and almond SKUs led by a marketing campaign targeting urban consumers. The launch is framed purely around consumer category growth, with no smallholder-sourcing or farming-systems component disclosed.

item.title: Vertical farming startup opens pilot facility in Singapore industrial park
item.description: A Singapore-based vertical farming startup has opened a 4,500 square-metre pilot facility in the Tuas industrial park, claiming yield parity with imported leafy greens. The operation is positioned as climate-smart precision agriculture and has no stated smallholder, cooperative, or regional supply-chain component.
